title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Guided Program Studio
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds unit-labeled 2D geometry model from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of unit-labeled 2D geometry model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Coordinate Convention
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 8603 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Coordinate Convention.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Units
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-110p98" that uses case 7590 to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Motion Units into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Motion Units, keep Motion Units connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-110p98
# Competency: asteroids-motion-units.
# Case 7590: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_units_110p98(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_110p98 = "110p98-89p011-7590"
    assert evidence_variant_110p98
    evidence_axes_110p98 = (
        "player110p98",
        "seed7590",
        "input89p011",
        "frame110p987590",
        "state7590110p98",
        "playtest89p0117590",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_110p98)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"
